full_text: In the Avatar comics, Aang consults Roku multiple times after making his promise to Zuko that he would end Zuko if he showed any signs of becoming like his father. So when Zuko makes multiple decisions that threaten the peace of the four nations, Aang calls Roku for advice, saying he didn't even end Ozai, how could he possibly consider ending his friend. Roku insists that Aang must keep his promise and expresses his own regret for not ending Fire Lord Sozin. When war is on the verge of starting between the Fire and Earth nation, Aang goes to meditate and talks to Roku who once again advocates for Aang to fulfill his promise and even reveals that Zuko is his great grandson. After the conflict is peacefully resolved, Aang talks to Roku one last time, thanking him for all he has taught him but claiming that this is a new kind of world and he cannot continue to rely on advice from those in the past. Aang says goodbye to Roku as he snaps off the totem with the Fire Nation sigil and burns it in his hand. This does permanent damage to Aang's ability to connect spiritually with the past avatars but later on another former avatar finds a different way to contact Aang.
